description: Add structured observability to the API using OpenTelemetry (OTel) as the vendor-neutral standard. Emit logs, traces, and metrics via OTLP and visualize them in Grafana using the Grafana LGTM stack (Loki, Grafana, Tempo, Mimir/Prometheus).

userStories.title: Add OTel dependencies and configure SDK
userStories.description: As a developer, I want OpenTelemetry configured in the FastAPI app so that traces and metrics are collected automatically. Add OTel packages to pyproject.toml. Create app/telemetry.py to initialize the OTel SDK with OTLP exporter, trace provider, and meter provider. Wire into FastAPI app startup. Include opentelemetry-instrumentation-sqlalchemy per design decision.
acceptanceCriteria: opentelemetry-api, opentelemetry-sdk, opentelemetry-exporter-otlp-proto-http, opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi, opentelemetry-instrumentation-httpx, opentelemetry-instrumentation-sqlalchemy are in pyproject.toml
app/telemetry.py exists and initializes TracerProvider, MeterProvider, and OTLP exporter
FastAPI auto-instrumentation is wired into app startup in app/main.py
OTel configuration reads from standard OTEL_* environment variables
Existing tests still pass (pytest -xvs)
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Include opentelemetry-instrumentation-sqlalchemy per design decision. Completed.

userStories.title: Add structured JSON logging with trace correlation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want structured JSON logs with trace/span ID correlation so that logs can be searched and linked to traces in Grafana. Configure Python logging with a JSON formatter. Add opentelemetry-instrumentation-logging to inject trace_id and span_id. Support per-module log levels via environment variables.
acceptanceCriteria: app/logging.py (or equivalent) configures JSON-formatted logging
Log output includes timestamp, level, message, trace_id, span_id, service.name fields
opentelemetry-instrumentation-logging is installed and configured
Per-module log level overrides work via LOG_LEVEL_<module>=<level> env vars
No print() statements remain in app code
Request logging middleware logs method, route, status code, and duration
Existing tests still pass (pytest -xvs)
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Per-module log levels per design decision. Completed.

userStories.title: Add Docker Compose for Grafana LGTM stack
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Docker Compose file that runs the Grafana LGTM stack locally so that I can view logs, traces, and metrics during development. Use the grafana/otel-lgtm all-in-one image.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.observability.yml exists with grafana/otel-lgtm service
Grafana is exposed on port 3000
OTLP endpoints exposed on ports 4317 (gRPC) and 4318 (HTTP)
.env.example includes all OTEL_* environment variables with sensible defaults
docker compose -f docker-compose.observability.yml up starts without errors

userStories.title: Create Grafana dashboard JSON provisioning
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a pre-built Grafana dashboard so that I can immediately view request rate, error rate, latency percentiles, logs, and traces without manual setup.
acceptanceCriteria: grafana/dashboards/api-overview.json exists with valid Grafana dashboard JSON
Dashboard includes panels: request rate by route, error rate (4xx/5xx), latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99), active requests gauge, recent logs (Loki), trace search (Tempo)
Grafana provisioning config auto-loads the dashboard on startup
docker-compose.observability.yml mounts the provisioning config

userStories.title: Add health check and readiness probe instrumentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a /health endpoint that is traced but excluded from latency metrics so that health check traffic does not skew p99 numbers. Add a readiness probe that verifies OTel collector connectivity.
acceptanceCriteria: /health endpoint exists and returns 200 with status JSON
/health is excluded from http.server.request.duration metrics
/health requests still generate traces (for debugging)
Readiness probe checks OTel collector connectivity
Tests cover /health endpoint behavior
Existing tests still pass (pytest -xvs)

userStories.title: Document observability setup in CLAUDE.md
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CLAUDE.md to document the observability setup so that anyone can start the stack, view dashboards, and add custom instrumentation.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md has an Observability section
Documents how to start the Grafana LGTM stack (docker compose command)
Lists all OTEL_* environment variables and their purpose
Explains how to view logs, traces, and metrics in Grafana
Explains how to add custom spans and metrics in code
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Last in chain — human review checkpoint.
